title: lichen-senml and lichen-oscore define standalone BufferTooSmall instead of using lichen-core error types
description:
lichen-schc and lichen-coap properly wrap lichen_core::error::{TooShort, BufferTooSmall} with From impls for error chaining. However:

- lichen-senml/src/cbor.rs defines its own CborError with BufferTooSmall as a unit variant
- lichen-oscore/src/lib.rs defines OscoreError::BufferTooSmall as a unit variant  
- lichen-rpl/src/message.rs defines RplError with TooShort and BufferTooSmall as unit variants

This breaks error chaining and loses diagnostic information (expected vs actual sizes).

What goes wrong: When debugging compression/encoding failures, callers cannot extract how many bytes were needed vs available. The inconsistency also means caller code cannot uniformly handle buffer errors across crates.
close_reason: Implemented wrapped TooShort/BufferTooSmall error types using lichen-core::error re-exports from lichen-ipv6; updated lichen-senml, lichen-oscore, and lichen-rpl to use the new wrapped types with proper size information
